---
title: "NOAA OISST"
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
using GeoDataAccess
using GeoDataAccess: DataAccessPlan, MetaData, NOAAOISST
using Dates
```

The [NOAA OISST](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/optimum-interpolation-sst) (Optimum
Interpolation Sea Surface Temperature) is a global daily SST analysis on a 0.25° grid.  It
blends observations from satellites (AVHRR), ships, buoys, and Argo floats into a consistent
climate data record from 1981 to present.

Each daily file contains four variables:

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `sst`  | Sea surface temperature (°C) |
| `anom` | SST anomaly from 30-year climatological mean (°C) |
| `ice`  | Sea ice concentration (%) |
| `err`  | Estimated SST error (°C) |

```{julia}
MetaData(NOAAOISST.Source())
```

## Basic Usage

```julia
using GeoInterface.Extents: Extent
using Dates

# Download a week of daily SST files
plan = DataAccessPlan(NOAAOISST.Source(),
    Extent(X=(-80.0, -60.0), Y=(30.0, 45.0)),
    Date(2024, 1, 1), Date(2024, 1, 7))
files = fetch(plan)

# SST and anomalies near a point
plan = DataAccessPlan(NOAAOISST.Source(), (-74.0, 40.7),
    Date(2024, 6, 1), Date(2024, 6, 3);
    variables = [:sst, :anom])
files = fetch(plan)
```

## Options

| Keyword | Type | Default | Description |
|---------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variables` | `Vector{Symbol}` | `[:sst]` | Variables of interest (all four are in every file) |
| `retention` | `Period` or `nothing` | `nothing` | Cache retention period |

::: {.callout-note}
Each daily file (~1.6 MB) contains the full global grid for all four variables.  The
`variables` keyword documents your intent but does not affect which data is downloaded.
Spatial subsetting happens at load time via `Rasters.jl`.
:::

## Loading Raster Data

`fetch` returns file paths to NetCDF files.  To load them as raster data, install the
`Rasters` package:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.add("Rasters")

using Rasters
files = fetch(plan)
r = Raster(files[1])
```

## Tips

- **File size**: Each daily file is ~1.6 MB.  A full year is ~580 MB.
- **Temporal range**: Data is available from September 1981 to present.
- **Version**: Downloads use OISST v2.1 (AVHRR-only), the current production version.
- **Citation**: Huang et al. (2020), NOAA 0.25° Daily OISST v2.1. DOI: [10.25921/RE9P-PT57](https://doi.org/10.25921/RE9P-PT57)
